summary: Scope is explicit, measurable, and enforced at the file-edit boundary — declared in a Scope Card, grounded in the right system first, and never crossed by direct edits into another layer or module.

rules.rule: Declare a Scope Card (SYSTEM/ROLE/DOMAIN/SCOPE/LAYER/FILES) before the first file edit; if the human did not specify a scope, ask — never guess — and never edit files outside the declared FILES glob without explicit human authorization.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: In a multi-system workspace, confirm which system you are in and how it actually runs before touching its build/dev/tests/infra, and before any destructive command (rm -rf, reset --hard, mass delete) confirm the repo identity with a cheap ground-check.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: A vertical agent edits horizontal files, or a horizontal agent edits vertical files — layers communicate through tasks, not direct edits.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Fix a cross-boundary need or an out-of-scope discovery yourself — write a TASK in the coordination lane for the layer owner, or a FINDING for the discovery, then continue your own work.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Before starting work, check the Assignment Registry: if another agent is assigned to the module do not start and ask the human; otherwise add yourself, and update your status when done.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Expand silently into another front, domain, or constitutional concern — say the topic shift explicitly and decide whether the current branch/worktree still fits or a new front must be opened.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
